name: Groundnut Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 jalapeno peppers, roasted and seeded, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet, fry the onion in peanut oil over medium heat until translucent.
2. Add the sliced tomatoes and roasted, seeded jalapeno peppers to the skillet. Cook for 10 minutes or until the vegetables are soft.
3. In a bowl, mix together the peanut butter, boiling water, and arrowroot until smooth.
4. Stir the black pepper into the mixture.
5. Pour the peanut paste over the cooked vegetables in the skillet.
6. Simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes to combine all flavors.
7. Makes 1 cup of sauce. Serve drizzled over teff cakes or use as desired.
8. The sauce will keep in the refrigerator for up to 1 week when stored in an airtight container.
